preview_text:
The model assesses Argentina as a strong favorite with an 84% win probability, compared to Egypt's 1% chance of victory. This asymmetry reflects a substantial gap in the forecast likelihood of each team's success. Egypt enters with minimal modeled odds of taking three points.

The draw probability sits at 16% under the model, suggesting the match carries a meaningful chance of stalemate but is shaped primarily around an Argentina triumph. The outcome distribution indicates a heavily one-sided contest in expectation, with little competitive tension forecasted between the two probabilities.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. Bookmakers assign Egypt a 10% win probability—roughly nine percentage points higher than the model—and credit Argentina with 70% rather than 84%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbook consensus on Egypt and draw outcomes. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the gap warrants attention for those tracking model performance against market prices.
